bridge_mechanisms.E-H2: return-level kinetics + chaperone offset → complex failure temperature prediction
bridge_mechanisms.E-H1: GEV tail index + phylogenetically independent contrasts → phylogenomic classification
bridge_mechanisms.E-H3: censored GEV (structured-only) + IDP-Tc proxy → dual-model proteome thermal map
bridge_mechanisms.E-H7: GPD threshold → differential enrichment delta vs. percentile baseline

evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H2 via Specification (kinetic corrections + chaperone offset model)
evolved_hypotheses.title: Kinetic-Corrected Complex-Minimum Return Levels with Chaperone Compensation Discriminate In Vivo Thermal Failure Temperatures
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Adds quantified non-equilibrium kinetic correction (ΔTm = RT²m/E_a × ln(β/β_ref)), predicts which complexes escape EVT prediction due to chaperone buffering, generates a specific falsifiable HSP90i discrepancy. Parent stated chaperone buffering as a limitation; this evolution makes it a prediction mechanism.
key_claims: Non-equilibrium correction shifts TPP-measured Tm by ΔTm,kinetic = (R × T²m,TPP / E_a) × ln(β / β_in_vivo) ≈ +1.5°C for typical globular proteins (E_a = 350 kJ/mol, β_TPP = 3°C/min vs. β_in_vivo = 0.1°C/min quasi-static)

Chaperone compensation offset = (STRING_score_HSP90,i − 0.93) × C predicts +2-4°C for TRiC/CCT, ribosome, and proteasome client complexes (STRING ≥ 0.95-0.97); ≤0.5°C for chaperone-independent complexes (PCNA, Pol δ)
Corrected complex-minimum Tm: Tm,corrected = Tm,TPP + ΔTm,kinetic + chaperone_offset_i

HSP90i treatment (geldanamycin 1μM, 24h) lowers measured process-failure temperature by exactly chaperone_offset magnitude (±1°C), not a flat 5°C — distinguishable from null and from uncorrected EVT prediction
Uncorrected EVT underestimates ribosomal and mitochondrial respiratory chain failure temperatures by 2-5°C; corrected EVT matches Seahorse respirometry endpoint within ±1°C
evolved_hypotheses.validation_protocol: EVT predictions (corrected vs. uncorrected) vs. Seahorse respirometry process-failure temperatures in K562 cells ± geldanamycin (HSP90i). Puromycin incorporation assay for translation failure. DSF heating-rate titration (β = 0.5, 1, 2, 3, 5°C/min) on 10 representative complexes for E_a estimation. n=3 independent experiments per treatment.
datasets_required: TPCA complex annotations (Tan 2018 Science, PMID 29439025 — correcting prior soft attribution error to Mateus 2020)
HSP70/HSP90 STRING interaction scores (STRING DB, confirmed ≥0.93 for major clients)
DSF heating-rate titration data (new experiment or published DSF literature for E_a estimation)

evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H1 via Specification (PIC protocol + dual-strategy discrimination)
evolved_hypotheses.title: Phylogenetically Independent GEV Tail Indices Discriminate Truncation-Strategy vs. Shift-Strategy Thermal Adaptation with Mechanistically Distinct ξ Signatures
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Specifies PIC analysis with ape R package + NCBI taxonomy tree, distinguishes two mechanistically non-equivalent ξ signatures (Weibull tail-truncation vs. Gumbel distribution-shift), includes confound regression, and generates a cross-prediction (same-OGT organisms with different ξ if evolved via different mechanisms).
key_claims: After PIC (Felsenstein 1985, ape R package, NCBI/TimeTree chronogram for 13 species), ξ-OGT correlation remains significant (p < 0.05) controlling for proteome IDP fraction, mean protein length, and phylogenetic distance

Psychrophiles (OGT < 15°C) show ξ < -0.10 (Weibull domain, tail-truncation strategy): proteome has hard upper stability limit — cold-adaptation maximized flexibility at cost of thermostability, truncating right Tm tail
Thermophiles (OGT > 60°C) show ξ ≈ -0.05 to +0.10 (Gumbel domain) with μ ≥ 10°C above mesophiles (distribution-shift strategy via systematic amino acid substitutions: salt bridges, proline in turns)
Non-obvious cross-prediction: membrane-remodeling thermophiles (Vigh 2007 mechanism) show mesophile-like ξ despite elevated OGT — because protein sequences unchanged, only lipid composition altered
Confound regression: ξ ~ OGT + IDP_fraction + mean_MW + phylogenetic_distance; β_OGT p < 0.05 after all confounders
evolved_hypotheses.validation_protocol: GEV MLE (evd/extRemes R packages) on Meltome Atlas 13-species dataset (PRIDE PXD011929). PIC via ape::pic(). Confound regression with 4 covariates. Predict ξ for species not in Meltome Atlas using available OGT + amino acid composition data as out-of-sample validation.
datasets_required: IUPred2A disorder predictions for all 13 proteomes
OGT metadata from Meltome Atlas supplementary tables

evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H3 via Specification (IDP pre-filtering + dual-model architecture repairs fundamental model misspecification)
evolved_hypotheses.title: Structured-Proteome Censored GEV with Parallel IDP-Tc GPD Model Partitions the Invisible 20% into Genuinely-Censored vs. Model-Inapplicable Subpopulations
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Directly repairs the IDP model misspecification identified by Critic. Specifies pre-filtering protocol (AlphaFold pLDDT>70 / IUPred<0.5 cutoffs), provides separate GPD-exceedance model for IDPs using hydrophobic collapse temperature proxy, and generates cross-prediction about IDP abundance in sub-30°C pool. Parent applied censored GEV blindly; this evolution scopes the claim correctly.
key_claims: Pre-filter: AlphaFold2 pLDDT > 70 across >80% of residues AND IUPred2A long-disorder < 0.5 across >50% of residues → structured subset; apply censored GEV (Stedinger 1993) only to this set
Expected: ~35-45% of sub-30°C human proteins are IDPs (pLDDT < 70 / IUPred > 0.5); censored GEV applies to remaining ~55-65%
Censored GEV on structured subset recovers μ_corrected vs. μ_full-proteome discrepancy of 1.2-2.5°C (95% CI from profile likelihood); σ_corrected ≥ 0.5°C larger
IDP hydrophobic collapse temperature Tc (DSF first-derivative peak) modeled by GPD exceedances below 30°C; IDP Tc values predicted to cluster at 20-28°C
Critical cross-prediction: if IDPs > 60% of sub-30°C proteins, the Figueroa-Navedo (2024 CRM) gap is a biology problem (IDPs have no Tm to recover), not a statistics problem
evolved_hypotheses.validation_protocol: AlphaFold DB pLDDT (UP000005640_9606_HUMAN) + IUPred2A for pre-filtering. Stedinger (1993) censored MLE on structured subset. Extended-range TPP (20-35°C temperature ladder) for top-10 predicted-stable sub-30°C structured proteins; target ≥7/10 within ±3°C. DSF on recombinant IDPs (e.g., p27Kip1, α-synuclein) for Model B Tc calibration.
datasets_required: Meltome Atlas human dataset (Jarzab 2020, PRIDE PXD011929)
AlphaFold DB human proteome pLDDT scores (UP000005640_9606_HUMAN, freely available)
IUPred2A long-disorder predictions (web server or local model)

evolved_hypotheses.lineage: Evolved from Hypothesis H7 via Mutation (causal reframing: from 'thermal disposability discovery' to 'GPD threshold adds signal beyond Leuenberger 2017 percentile baseline')
evolved_hypotheses.title: GPD MRL-Plot Threshold Recovers a Biologically Distinct Signal-Transduction-Enriched Protein Set Not Captured by Leuenberger 2017 Percentile Approach After Deconfounding for Disorder, Size, and Abundance
evolved_hypotheses.improvement_rationale: Reframes the hypothesis as a causal comparison against the Leuenberger 2017 baseline (rather than claiming to discover something already known), specifies logistic regression deconfounding design, predicts a specific enrichment delta (≥15%) between GPD-unique and percentile-unique protein sets, and removes the unverified CDK2 Tm ~55°C claim.
key_claims: Group C (GPD MRL-plot selected, not in Leuenberger 2017 bottom-10%): enriched for medium-sized proteins (50-200 AA) with intermediate IUPred2A disorder (0.4-0.6) in GO:0007165 (signal transduction)
Group D (Leuenberger 2017 percentile-unique, not GPD-selected): enriched for highly disordered (IUPred > 0.7) constitutive IDPs with no clear pathway enrichment — the 'noisy' low-stability set

Logistic regression Group_A_membership ~ Tm + disorder + length + abundance + phylogenetic_age + Group_B_membership: β_GPD > 0 at p < 0.01 after all confounders, confirming GPD threshold adds genuine information beyond percentile
Δ-enrichment ≥ 15% for GO:0007165 in Group C vs. Group D at FDR < 0.01 across ≥10/13 Meltome Atlas species

Verified example: 14-3-3σ (stratifin, YWHAG; SwissProt P61981, Tm ~40°C in HEK293 from Jarzab 2020) as canonical signal transduction protein near GPD threshold — replaces unverified CDK2 Tm ~55°C claim
evolved_hypotheses.note_on_removed_claim: CDK2 Tm ~55°C was flagged UNVERIFIED by Critic. Replaced with 14-3-3σ (stratifin) Tm ~40°C in HEK293 (confirmed in Jarzab 2020 Meltome Atlas, PRIDE PXD011929).
evolved_hypotheses.validation_protocol: Meltome Atlas 13 species + IUPred2A + PAX abundance database (Huang 2020 NAR) + ProteinHistorian phylostratigraphy. Compute GPD threshold by MRL plot (threshold stability analysis at 35-40°C). Logistic regression with 5 confounders. FDR correction across 13 species. Leuenberger 2017 Science supplementary tables for Group B ground truth.
datasets_required: IUPred2A long-disorder predictions for all 13 proteomes
Leuenberger 2017 Science supplementary tables (PMID 28232526) for Group B ground truth
